facade_colour: warm brown/tan brick; newly constructed wooden porch in light wood

hcd_data.architectural_style: House-form, Detached, Ontario Cottage (confirmed – HCD Plan Vol. 2)
hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: The property has value as a nineteenth-century Ontario Cottage House-form building that reflects early workers’ housing within the District. Through its construction date and typology, the property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. The property has been modified but retains the original composition of a central gable and door flanked by a window to either side. Contextually, the property shares a consistent setback with the other contributing properties on the street. The property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 1 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form, detached, ontario cottage typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double hung, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Contextually, the property shares a consistent setback with the other contributing properties on the street. The property has been modified but retains the original composition of a central gable and door flanked by a window to either side.

facade_description: Detached single-storey Ontario cottage in warm brown/tan brick; newly constructed wooden porch in light wood with a gable roof. Features include simple bargeboards and a simple cornice. Porch recently reconstructed; some replacement windows; original form and brick retained. Pre-1889 construction.
heritage_summary: Contextually, the property shares a consistent setback with the other contributing properties on the street. The property has been modified but retains the original composition of a central gable and door flanked by a window to either side.
